show.title: Soulection Radio Show #173
show.content:
Joe Kay makes it to the studio in time to throw down an unforgettable set full of primarily forgotten classics that will have you reminiscing on life.

<!--more-->

As if this news isn't enough, we just hit 100K here on Soundcloud this past Friday literally while we launched our new residency @ the legendary Echoplex! Talk about timing and accomplishments. THANK you to each and every one of you for supporting us and spreading this sound to everyone. We plan to give you something really soon to celebrate 100K supporters worldwide and more. Big ups to the 900+ people who showed up to The Sound of Tomorrow this past Friday. Very inspiring.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #172 w/ Rhettmatic (Beat Junkies)
show.content:
Rhettmatic of the legendary beat Junkies Crew graces us with a 50 minute set + an in depth interview. 

<!--more-->

Rhett is definitely one of the most humble guests we've had on here and is more connected than anyone really knows. Do yourself a favor and get mentally prepared to learn a lot. It's much more than just music. Shouts to Rhettmatic, Mr. Choc, J. Rocc, and the rest of the Beat Junkies crew. Also, big big up to Free The Robots for coming through the studio and vibing with us. Much love to you & the whole Crosby crew. Stay tuned for much more to come. Enjoy.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #167
show.content:
Take a deep breath and slow things down with Joe Kay
<!--more-->

So many good things have been going on in our world but sometimes it is overwhelming. However we just take a deep breath and keep it moving. This week Joe Kay wanted to slow things down a bit and just focus on the music by going solo. Joe started off with some nice neo-soul/Dilla-esque vibes and then progressed into some progressive beats to get your energy flowing. Standout artists to look out for are LOSCO. These guys have a heavy, heavy sound. Just wait til you hear. As the show progressed the engagement via social media was massive and a few people randomly requested to hear a Sango tribute so we did one! Joe dropped a 42 minute set of primarily unreleased music to keep things interesting! We hope you enjoy the show and feel free to spread it to as many people as possible. Real radio is here.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #156 w/ J-Louis
show.content:
Joe Kay is joined by Soulection's very own iPhone producer J-Louis.

<!--more-->
As some of you may or may not know, J-Louis produces all his music on the iPhone [(Nano Studio App)](https://apps.apple.com/gb/developer/blip-interactive-ltd/id382263654?at=1010lMz4). He even put out a free EP with us in the beginning of the year entitled ["Balanced"](https://soulection.bandcamp.com/album/balanced-ep) which was also produced entirely on the iPhone. This 20 year-old from Azusa, CA is really a special guy who we feel is going to be BIG in the near future as long as he stays on track with his progression.

J-Louis played an exclusive set for a little over 30 minutes with all his recent unreleased productions and then chopped it up with us about his musical background (Drummer since he was 8 years-old & played in a Hardcore band & toured when he was 12) and how he purchased a stolen Macbook Pro (without knowing) to start producing on a computer that was eventually stolen -- forcing him to produce on his iPhone again. 

What a journey! Joe Kay also made sure to talk about his experience at the Yeezus tour this past Friday and later went into a heavy discussion about rumors of Soulection being part of Free-masonry/Illuminati. Very interesting and informative conversations going on as always.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #137 w/ Mr. Carmack & Mikos Da Gawd
show.content:
Mikos Da Gawd and Mr. Carmack each drop amazing sets of unreleased, original productions.

<!--more-->

Joe Kay kicked things off for the first half of the show! He dropped a lot of exclusives and forgotten gems. Mikos Da Gawd dropped a 30+ minute DJ set that consisted of many gems and original productions from the man himself. Mr. Carmack wrapped up the show with an amazing listening session of all of his latest unreleased works. Man, all we gotta say is that Carmack is one of the best in the game right now. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #144 w/ starRo
show.content:
starRo hails all the way from Tokyo, Japan to play original productions and edits.

<!--more-->

There have been so many guests on Soulection Radio over the past years but no one, we repeat, no one has came in the studio so humble, so low key and just murk the airwaves like starRo did. 
It's crazy to think that this man has been making music for over 15 years and is barely coming to surface to the world with so much heat. 
We are just glad he is on our team. He played nothing but original production/edits. 
Hailing all the way from Tokyo, Japan, starRo is an artist who you will be familiar with starting from this point on. 
Joe Kay of course opened the show for the first hour & 50 minutes on one turntable (one of the turntable's went out before the show started) playing a bunch of heat that he was holding onto since last week. 
He made sure to make up for his absence last saturday and play some exclusive Carmack, Atu, Kaytra, Esta, Esgar, Jo Def + some other heavy footwork vibes.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #146
show.content:
Joe Kay hits hard with 3 hours of HEAVY tracks on 1 hour of sleep

<!--more-->


There is something for everybody in here. Lots of unreleased music + music that was forgotten about. Shouts to all the amazing people who tuned in to the live show. Every once in a while we like to make a post about the location of our radio listeners and it's always inspiring to see how many people are actually tuning in and the locations that they reside in. We can't even pronounce some of the cities/countries! Talk about worldwide. Lets keep it moving.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #147 w/ DJ Nobody
show.content:
We have Low End Theory's very own DJ Nobody in the studio with us. 

<!--more-->
We hope you're ready for not just another great session of musical selections but also a great 25+ minute discussion between DJ Nobody and Joe Kay. 
Joe kicked things off for a little over an hour & 33 minutes with new and forgotten gems. 

Do your research! Nobody made his 1st appearance on the show that was well overdue and went into a heavy/experimental beat journey ranging a variety of BPM's. 
We discussed a bit of his background and really got into the history of Low End Theory and the progression of where it is today. 
Nobody discussed crucial experiences that you would want to know so listen closely. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #148
show.content:
Continuing Joe Kay's 24th birthday celibration with precise selections that you either forgot about or never heard.

<!--more-->

In celebration of Joe's 24th birthday yesterday, we started celebrating on Thursday at The Sound of Tomorrow (The Crosby) with Astronautica & starRo! Thank you to everyone who came out and supported, what a show it was. This radio session is just a continuation of the celebration with precise selections that you either forgot about or never heard. 
Bless to those who tuned into the live broadcast via Kbeach. It's amazing to think about how far we've came. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #151 w/ Mr. Choc (Beat Junkies)
show.content:
For this week's special guest if you want to talk about pioneers in the game [Mr. Choc](https://twitter.com/MrChocFan4) is one of them. 

<!--more-->

One of our favorite DJ's who's talent and accomplishments are not to be overlooked. 
Representing the world famous Beat Junkies crew and Skam Artists, the crew was blessed to have this man come by Kbeach and hit us with a set and great interview session. 
The thing we admire the most about Choc is that he has his ears close to the new generation of music and understands the culture. 
He threw down a really diverse set and kept us on our toes. 

Joe Kay was on his soulful chop vibe and has really been digging back into the classic neo-soul/boombap sound. 
Make sure you really pay attention to the interview at the end. 
Some great insight on commercial radio on the west coast as well as opportunities for upcoming DJ's. 



Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #154
show.content:
Joe Kay really displayed a crazy selection of music that will have you copying/pasting consistently within Google for some time. 

<!--more-->

There are a lot of new tunes sprinkled with the 'feel-good' classics that we always connect to our sets. It's essential for us to keep educating you with the influences and how it all connects to where we are today. Spread this set to as many people as possible! More minds and ears need to hear this music!

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #159 w/ Daddy Kev
show.content:
Kev dropps a 40 minute set that makes you feel like you're in the "2001: A Space Odyssey" space hub.

<!--more-->

This week's edition of Soulection Radio was really essential. We had a pioneer and important individual who is responsible for many great things going on in our music culture today. 
He goes by Daddy Kev which most of you may already be familiar with. He founded the legendary weekly event Low End Theory and co-founded Alpha Pup Records/Distribution. 

Kev made sure to sprinkle all the classics as well as some forthcoming unreleased material from who knows who. 

Things got really interesting when Kev and Joe jumped in to the interview portion during the 2nd hour to discuss everything from experiences at Low End Theory, 
mastering (sound engineering education), the current state of today's music culture, favorite artists to work with, and even a Low End Theory festival that is coming this year. 
Definitely an educational experience! 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #162
show.content:
Joe reaches out to the listeners for the People Choice

<!--more-->

Coming off Grammy week connections and our 3 Year Anniversary we returned for yet another solid show. Our special guests who were scheduled for this show couldn't make it but Joe Kay brought out a variety of new and classic gems from the Soulection crates. This show was really engaging because Joe reached out to all the listeners and asked the audience what they wanted to hear via social media. It was amazing to see how many people were listening and how many people requested for a certain track. For the last hour and 1/2 you the people we're DJ'ing/selecting music! How amazing right? Try to tune in live next time. The live feelings is always better. We'll catch you next week at the same time. Spread this if you feel it because it really is strictly about the music. Stay tuned for our 3 Year Anniversary Party announcement on Monday (1/27/14).


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #168 (SXSW Pre Show)
show.content:
Joe kay getting you ready for SXSW.
<!--more-->

As we are getting ready for SXSW we wanted to get you in the spirit. Joe Kay went solo again to keep things simple and focused. He kicked it off with the new Carmack/Esta collab, played some new discoveries/unreleased gems, and then switched up the whole format by letting the live listener selects the music by sending us our requests via our social media networks. What a beautiful thing! For over 2 hours, you had the chance to selects the music, Joe was just the provider! What a great way to collaborate right? Since we will be at SXSW from (March 11-16) there won't be a live Soulection Radio show next week, however, we do have a special guest mix to make up for it. Stay tuned.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection All Day 2021

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #138 w/ Great Dane
show.content:
Producer Great Dane (co-founder of Team Supreme) delivers energy and bass

<!--more-->


For this show we had the young and progressive producer Great Dane who is the co-founder of one of the best collectives in LA (Team Supreme) and an artist on Alpha Pup. Joe Kay kicked things off for the first hour & 28 minutes of exclusive material ranging from Mr. Carmack, Kaytranada, Sango, Jo Def, Esta, and many others. Great Dane truly understands our memo when we say, 'NO TIME FOR FILLER TRACKS'. As soon as he hit play constant energy and bass started flowing. He played an exclusive 45 minute set which consisted of all original productions except for two tracks. This won't be the last time you hear from him.
 



 Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #150 w/ SOSUPERSAM
show.content:
Talented & vibrant woman SOSUPERSAM finally came through the studio. 

<!--more-->
It's been a long time coming and we're happy that you finally get a feel for who she is musically and as a person. Wasn't quite sure what she was gonna play for us, but she showed a great diversity and really brought us back to a place that was very much needed towards the end of her set. She's even cooler as a person. Being humble and driven will always get you far. Joe Kay really brought out a diverse selection of music. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #143
show.content:
Joe Kay focuses playing dropping just over 100 tracks in 3 hours 

<!--more-->

It's been about 3 months or more since there has been no guest on Soulection Radio. 
It's been an amazing journey of having so many different personalities roll through the station and educate us with their production, selections, knowledge, and experiences. 
This week Joe Kay wanted to let loose and focus strictly on his selection. Within 3 hours Joe dropped just over 100 tracks, that's right over 100! 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #168 (SXSW Pre Show)
show.content:
Joe kay getting you ready for SXSW.
<!--more-->

As we are getting ready for SXSW we wanted to get you in the spirit. Joe Kay went solo again to keep things simple and focused. He kicked it off with the new Carmack/Esta collab, played some new discoveries/unreleased gems, and then switched up the whole format by letting the live listener selects the music by sending us our requests via our social media networks. What a beautiful thing! For over 2 hours, you had the chance to selects the music, Joe was just the provider! What a great way to collaborate right? Since we will be at SXSW from (March 11-16) there won't be a live Soulection Radio show next week, however, we do have a special guest mix to make up for it. Stay tuned.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #167
show.content:
Take a deep breath and slow things down with Joe Kay
<!--more-->

So many good things have been going on in our world but sometimes it is overwhelming. However we just take a deep breath and keep it moving. This week Joe Kay wanted to slow things down a bit and just focus on the music by going solo. Joe started off with some nice neo-soul/Dilla-esque vibes and then progressed into some progressive beats to get your energy flowing. Standout artists to look out for are LOSCO. These guys have a heavy, heavy sound. Just wait til you hear. As the show progressed the engagement via social media was massive and a few people randomly requested to hear a Sango tribute so we did one! Joe dropped a 42 minute set of primarily unreleased music to keep things interesting! We hope you enjoy the show and feel free to spread it to as many people as possible. Real radio is here.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #192 Ft. J. Rocc
show.content:
J. Rocc comes through to the Soulection HQ to drop a exclusive mix and a interview. 

<!--more-->
Over the years there have been a handful of artists/DJs that I have always wanted to come on the show. We've had Dam Funk, Mr. Choc, Daddy Kev, Hazel, Rhettmatic, Ta-ku, Coleman, Iman Omari, and many others as special guests but I've always wanted to have J. Rocc of the legendary Beat Junkies/Stones Throw crew on the show. Well, our timing finally aligned and he came through the Soulection HQ to drop a set and sit down with me for an interview. He dropped a 60 min set full of future beats, bass, and some other left-field shit.

After that we sat down and chopped it about so many interesting things from his love for digging for 45's (Gospel Music), his production (that he keeps low key), his DILLA/MADLIB stories, and much more. J. Rocc is an overall tastemaker and one of the best on the decks. If you ever have a chance to see him live please go to a show and see for yourselves.

Enjoy this one and spread the word.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #147 w/ DJ Nobody
show.content:
We have Low End Theory's very own DJ Nobody in the studio with us. 

<!--more-->
We hope you're ready for not just another great session of musical selections but also a great 25+ minute discussion between DJ Nobody and Joe Kay. 
Joe kicked things off for a little over an hour & 33 minutes with new and forgotten gems. 

Do your research! Nobody made his 1st appearance on the show that was well overdue and went into a heavy/experimental beat journey ranging a variety of BPM's. 
We discussed a bit of his background and really got into the history of Low End Theory and the progression of where it is today. 
Nobody discussed crucial experiences that you would want to know so listen closely. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

Soulection Radio Show #369 w/Yukibeb
show.content:
Joe Kay meets up with Japanese DJ [Yukibeb](https://twitter.com/yukibeb) drops a 60-minutes guest mix live in Tokyo. --- Today we're introducing the Black Label Series, which is focused on highlighting rising vocalists/rappers we're going to be working with. As smooth as they are soulful, LA-based group Hablot Brown gives us 5 original tracks that will grab you by the heartstrings. From the vocals to guitar-infused production, the first ever Black Label release will make it hard for you to not follow their journey. Discover:
[soundcloud.com/hablotbrown](https://soundcloud.com/hablotbrown)
[twitter.com/hablotbrown](https://twitter.com/hablotbrown)
[instagram.com/hablotbrown/](https://instagram.com/hablotbrown/) ### Credits Released July 29th, 2018 Mixed & Mastered by Joshua Pleeter & Austin Brown Artwork: Andre Power

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #149 w/ House Shoes
show.content:
The pioneer/legendary House Shoes kicks off exclusives from label Street Corner Music.

<!--more-->

This man's musical selections, experiences/knowledge, and blunt personality is one that you can't deny. 

We respect House Shoes! It was nice to finally have him come by and drop two different sets (that's right two!). 

He kicked it off with exclusives from his new label Street Corner Music and then ended with some nice soulful joints. 

Joe Kay's discussion with Shoes also touched on many great subjects that many of you wanted to know. Enjoy.



Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #146
show.content:
Joe Kay hits hard with 3 hours of HEAVY tracks on 1 hour of sleep

<!--more-->


There is something for everybody in here. Lots of unreleased music + music that was forgotten about. Shouts to all the amazing people who tuned in to the live show. Every once in a while we like to make a post about the location of our radio listeners and it's always inspiring to see how many people are actually tuning in and the locations that they reside in. We can't even pronounce some of the cities/countries! Talk about worldwide. Lets keep it moving.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #517 (Golden Era of SoundCloud: 2008-2017 Pt. I)
show.content:

show.title: Soulection All Day 2023
show.content:
**🎊🌟 Happy New Year 2023 🌟🎊**

The 5th year in a row Soulection Takesover Apple Music with of exclusive mixes from [Joe Kay](/episodes/all-day-2023/#joe-kays-set), [Jeftuz](/episodes/all-day-2023/#jeftuzs-set), [1981 Tokyo](/episodes/all-day-2023/#1981-tokyos-set), and [S!RENE](/episodes/all-day-2023/#srenes-set).

<!--more-->

#### Jump to:

- [Joe Kay](/episodes/all-day-2023/#joe-kays-set)
- [Jeftuz](/episodes/all-day-2023/#jeftuzs-set)
- [1981 Tokyo](/episodes/all-day-2023/#1981-tokyos-set)
- [S!RENE](/episodes/all-day-2023/#srenes-set)

Also listen to [Soulection All Day 2022](/episodes/all-day-2022/), [Soulection All Day 2021](/episodes/all-day-2021), [Soulection All Day 2020](/episodes/all-day-2020), and [Soulection All Day 2019](/episodes/all-day-2019).
